assertions.description: The analyzer detects J1 (USB_C_USB2.0) and runs 7 compliance checks. CC1/CC2 5.1k pull-downs pass, USB ESD IC passes, but vbus_esd_protection and vbus_decoupling fail. DP/DM series resistors are informational. The summary reports 3 pass, 2 fail, 2 info. This is an accurate characterization of a USB-C device-only (sink) implementation that has ESD protection IC but lacks a dedicated VBUS ESD TVS diode and bulk decoupling on the VBUS rail at the connector.

The analyzer detects U2 (CAT24M01X, 1Mbit EEPROM) and U3 (BL24C256A, 256kbit EEPROM) as memory interfaces. Both are correctly identified as connected to both U1 (CY7C68013A USB MCU) and U30 (ICE40HX8K FPGA) via 2 shared signal nets (I2C SDA/SCL). This is the Glasgow debug tool's I2C bus architecture where the MCU and FPGA share access to both configuration EEPROMs.
